park_name: Snug Beach Cabin & Caravan Tourist Park

reviews.review_text: Only stayed in the non-powered sites, but loved it. Great location, beautiful sunrises. Showers and toilets are cleanand tidy. The common lounge\kitchen was a welcome surprise, making washups easy and if the weather was inclement you cloud escape here. The booking process was smooth and  easily accomplished. We arrived after hours and all the info we needed was provided. I think the price was reasonable for the amenities provided.

We had an overnight stay in one of the standard studio cabins . While compact ,it was well appointed with everything you might need . The cabin was very clean and the bed ( with a much appreciated electric blanket) was very comfy. Reception staff were very friendly and accommodating re a possible late check in . Camp kitchen was really quite unique with a blazing fireplace , very cosy . . Located right on the beach. . Highly recommended.
We really enjoyed our stay at Snug. Very good facilities and an excellent IGA up the road.
Camping très agréable avec un emplacement en bord de mer très joli
Great spot, right on the beach. Really good camp kitchen with indoor and outdoor seating, lounge, TV, and everything else you need including a snacks vending machine and book exchange.

Booked a van site for 3 days under the belief that it was on Brunie Island. After booking arrived 2 hrs later only to find out that it wasn’t Brunnie Island. Asked if I would possibly able to get a refund as I only booked 2 hrs earlier. Was told no I could have 2 of the three days as they could have sold that extra day. Remembering I had only booked 2 hrs earlier. Asked if I could transfer the night I had to pay for to another night when we came back to snug beach. Was told no again, they wouldn’t be able to accommodate us. Would I recommend this place. Not a chance, there customer service has a lot to be desired.

We loved our 2 stay night here, spacious unpowered site, we had ducks come visit and a Bandicoot which was pretty cool, lovely heated kitchen area where you can relax out of the cold. BBQs inside, microwave etc. good size showers and clean, except you have to leave them run for a while for hot water to come in (bit of a waste of water). Would come back in a heartbeat, quiet, friendly, a well stocked iga really close, we walked up. Loved it, almost stayed a third night.

Absolutely awesome van park, great location and the laundry dialysis in your card and and spit the coins out for your washing and drying. Great owners, couldn't be more helpful. Highly recommended, we keep extending our stay 😁😁
Great place to stay

Stay here for 1 night with powered site for the rental camper, morning can see lot of birds. Is convenient location after visit to Brunny island, theres grocery, fast food, petrol station all nearby here.

Great caravan park right on the beach. Very friendly staff, large grassy sites and fantastic amenities. Only 10 mins to the Bruny Island ferry and IGA at the end of the street make it very convenient.
Little gem of a place.

Snug!!!!!What a wonderful place to stay in the Cabin and Caravan Park.
Reception Staff were terrific, explaining all the sights in the area. With Snug being 30 minutes by car from Hobart CBD and the hike in petrol prices, staff informed us of the Park and Ride from Huntingfield (15 minutes away) being pensioners our fare was $1.20 p/p per trip!!!!!!! Our cabin was very clean and comfy. (Home away from home) amenities terrific. My husband and I would highly recommend this park
Disappointed that a refund was declined on a cancellation when it was obvious that park was almost at capacity.

The owner Amy needs a serious lesson in customer service,  we arrived late, just after 4pm when the office closes, limping in without  battery power. My daughter had tried to call to book a spot but got message bank. I am 80  and was happy to arrive somewhere where we might be able to power up. Amy was seriously rude and unhelpful even after her partner Brad suggested the emergency overflow spot, Amy did not want to accommodate us. Luckily Brad pressed on and  we managed to get the spot after being left feeling seriously unwelcome and unwanted. Marijke

Mum (80) and I arrived at 5.30 pm thursday afternoon, I had tried calling but  office closes at 4pm. We were in a bit of a pickle as we had come from Campbelltown and had discovered our batteries were not charging at all, so we were looking for a powered site, as the auto- mechanic had suggested (which we had seen in Hobart)  |Of course upon arrival the office was closed but I rang the bell as was suggested on the notice beside it. I was met by Amy the manager who was  in no mood to help me and was actually seriously rude. Luckily her partner Brad was very helpful and piped up that there was an overflow spot, Amy said to Brad she did not really want us there, but Brad said it was there for emergencies, |Amy stomped off angrily and Brad  helped me further. Brad was seriously helpful and I couldn't have wished for better , but his partner Amy has some serious issues.

I was in studio A and although it’s cute, the bed was not the best and I had a sore back the next day. Through no fault of their own the park was close to a house that was partying until 12am and it was off putting. The staff were great. The place was a bit smaller they expected and the online site price gouged around $180 for 1 night. I could have bought the night for cheaper by contacting the hotel but didn’t think to

Park was close to town and right by the beach. Lady at reception was extremely helpful with local area and also to help with directions as we needed repairs to van. Amenities were clean and camp kitchen was great.
Wonderful campsite. Lots of space, free showers (for patrons) access to the beach, lovely clean amenities and a super friendly wholesome vibe!
태즈매니아 도착해서 첫 숙소로 캐러밴 자리 이용하였습니다! 샤워실 따뜻한 물 잘 나오고 부엌, 화장실 등 쾌적하게 잘 되어있습니다. 비가 온 뒤라 크고 예쁜 무지개까지 봐서 너무 좋았습니다.
Stayed here for three nights, cabin was very clean and spacious. Staff were fantastic could not do enough for you . We would highly recommend it. Peter and Toni.

We enjoyed  our stay greatly.  Love being  near the beach. Staff members  great. Showers were free. Had dog bath area. Showers and toilet  area great. Will return. Love meeting people  and dogs. Visitors aloud, that's great. Thank you.

We used this place for caravan parking but it also has really well structured cabins that one can use if travelling in a car.
Its one of the few pet friendly places with a dog wash. The facilities are well maintained - toilets are clean, well lit and bathrooms are spacious with proper hot and cold water supply. Highlight was the kitchen area which has a lot of comfortable seating spaces, a library and board game section too. There is also a bbq station and playing equipments that you can rent without any charge. Overall a great experience and great location just next to a beach.
This place does not adjust sites to cater for mobility impaired people. Requested a site close to amenities block, due to mobility impairment. Got put in a site 100m away. Rubbish!
Great spot.
Camped in very family friendly area.

It’s quite a beautiful holiday park located at Snug. Good access to beach. Good spacious powered and unpowered sites. Clean toilet and showers . Just I wish it had more cooking stuff more stoves ovens fryers cups cutlery. It was a bit hard to take all these stuff with you to the kitchen for every meal.

We loved our time at Snug Caravan Park! Very friendly staff. We camped unpowered and loved being so close to the water and off lead dog section. Facilities were good. Great library available for all the book worms. Fantastic location to explore Cygnet, Hobart and Bruny.
Could not thank Amy enough for finding us room for our caravan as they are so full, Amy went right out of her way to assist us in every way 🙏 thank you Amy 🙏 all the best

Ideal location as a base for local drives and access to Bruny Island ferry|Cabin was very roomy and comfortable and cooking facilities were great|Local walk includes good memorial to bushfires that wiped out town a few years ago

The beachfront location at Snug Beach Cabin & Caravan Tourist Park is absolutely stunning. My cabin was clean, cozy, and had everything I needed for a relaxing getaway. The staff were friendly and helpful, making my stay effortless. Wake-up views of the ocean were incredible, and the nearby beach access is perfect for morning walks.

Old cabins in need of work.
The price does not reflect what they are worth..
Gorgeous little caravan park with cabins and powered and non powered sites, friendly manager and staff

Visited this campground recently in a motorhome and had a great experience. It had everything we needed - powered sites, clean facilities, hot showers and a laundry. Lovely location beside the beach and a nice nearby memorial to the devastating bush fires in 1967. Would recommend this site :)

Felt quite at home in this little park nestled by the beach. The staff have been very helpful and friendly and the park’s facilities were perfect for our needs. There isn’t a bouncy pillow or playground but the children  in the park seemed very happy running around with each other. |It’s about a half hour to Hobart and great access to the southern areas or inland and Bruny Island ferry close by.|My husband did fish a few times but no luck.

We stayed here for a few nights as a base for Salamanca Markets, Mt Wellington, Snug Falls, Cygnet and Huonville area. Clean showers and Camp Kitchen and friendly/ helpful owners. Great value and nice quiet area. Thank you.

We stayed in a cabin our travell friends had their motorhome.
Lots of room in the cabin 2bdrm, good kitichen, plenty of cookware
River walk accross the road
Great place to camp, clean and well maintained amenities and grounds. Very easy to book and throughly enjoyed our time here.  Highly recommend for a peaceful and lovely stay.

Excellent place to stay for travellers.  The staff has been so supportive and helpful when we experience car difficulties. Their kindness is so appreciated.  The facilities are excellent and the views of the ocean were great.  Great place to stay if you are exploring the southern part of Tasmania.
Superbe camping

Super friendly staff and family operated. They were helpful and ensured our whole group was near each other. There’s a nice shared area with tables, a beautiful fireplace, tv, kitchen, BBQs, kids toys, books and couches. Just a jump over to the beach!
Is a great Caravan and Cabin Park. Managers are awesome.  Very good facilities,  short walk to the beach.
Beautiful and quite spot tucked away near snugg beach. The staff are super friendly and  accommodating. Couldn’t recommend this spot enough!
Great friendly service
What an awesome place! Best location, cabins are great and the staff are amazing ! 10 stars from me!

Used a powered site can't comment on the service in person as we had an after hours check-in at 4pm with details left in a drop box, extended our stay by online booking was seamless. Very clean park, kitchen had a fire going every night,bathrooms were great. There are also about 6 ducks that live in the park who were glad to be fed.
Great cabin. Very clean. Very modern.  Everything you need for 1 night or a week. Stove, oven full size fridge. Definatly recommended.

We had a nice takeaway picnic on the picnic tables at Snug Beach, then a walk on the beach.  It was quiet and relaxing but also cosy.  The new toilet facilities were great.  We did not stay at the caravan park, but it has a lovely location.
Stayed in the cabins. Outdated and cramped however it was clean and well stocked up. Good stay
Great spot for a base when exploring the chanell region.  Clean ,affordable ,and friendly.
The van park was probably the best one we have stayed at in Tasmania. Fantastic facilities and well maintained sites. 10 out of 10

TERRIBLE customer service - RUDE & OBNOXIOUS owners.
Dirty communal showers & kitchen.  Signs in kitchen [loud & abrupt] are a reflection of the way the place is run!

Owners are - RUDE, unreasonable & extremely combative! ||They have ZERO customer service skills & are 'nice' until they take your money......beware!! ||Filthy communal showers & kitchen area - signs in kitchen are 'loud & aggressive' [no basic  manners or polite requests]  - a reflection on how the place is run.||Happy to take your money [you even have to pay for a log to throw on the communal fire] - but don't expect to reason with them or make any requests!
Very nice amenities & friendly staff

The staff are so lovely, the camp kitchen is great, no limit on showers you just need to mop them out when your done, the place is very scenic, not many shells if that is what you are looking for (which is what I look for) but for a complete relax it’s a great place to go.  Our camp site fitted a 10 person tent and gazebo so we were happy.
Lovely  well set out caravan park.  Great amenities.  A few metres to the lovely beach .
Very helpful managers (they came back to let us in when we arrived after closing time), clean facilities,  wood fire in the communal kitchen. If you like caravan parks,  this is a great one

Nice caravan park and very close and accessible to the beach. A great spot for families and for children to play on the beach.|Close to supermarket. The shower and toilets are clean.|Big laundry and plenty of clothes lines.|Park is full of ducks

We were in a van so can't comment on rooms. Lovely grass site, footsteps from the beach. Fantastic camp kitchen,  free electric BBQs, toasters, microwave,  electric cooktops, huge fridge, and the best fire with complimentary wood!.
Possibly the best caravan park we have stayed in. If visiting Hobart,  this is a perfect spot, only 30 minutes to the city. Will definitely be back.
We stayed in our caravan

Stayed in the unpowered camping section and i can’t rate the property high enough. Location is perfect with beach access, owners are accomodating ( i booked in at very very short notice) facilities are above any i’ve stayed at across tasmania so far and there’s a lot. Laundry room has a coin change / card purchase facility which is super convenient! Highly recommend

We were booked in here for three nights and hubby has done his back in Queenstown. I rang the morning when we were due to arrive and had to cancel as I can’t get him in the car. Amy told me she would still have to charge me one night unless she could sell the site. She charged my card the $50. I got my brother in law to call at 4pm and was told they had no powered sites left. I rang Amy back and she said no they had not sold the site and when I told her what my brother in law was just told, she very smartly told me they closed at 4 and they weren’t on site to sell anything. ||She is a liar. They close at 5pm and I could also hear someone else in the back ground checking in people. Great service Amy. I hope you never have a medical mishap on your travels, and if you do you are treated the same as you treat your customers, with tone, attitude and you are a liar!

UPDATE: so now I get an email explaining their terms and conditions! Yes I understand all that! BUT MY POINT IS…….to be told if she sells the site she will refund me the $50! My brother in law when called was told they had no power sites left. So obviously she sold the site……..I have no refund! You are a liar Amy despite what you want to call it. Everywhere is booked out at the moment, and I’m sure they were too!

The time in your voice when I called you and the the way you virtually laughed at me when I said you just told my brother in law you had no sites left, you need some training in customer service Amy. I’m sure you would live on site, but you told me you weren’t on site when I called you that’s why you had nothing to sell. You need to back up what you say! Another lie!

We were booked in here for three nights and hubby has done his back in Queenstown. I rang the morning when we were due to arrive and had to cancel as I can’t get him in the car. Amy told me she would still have to charge me one night unless she could sell the site. She charged my card the $50. I got my brother in law to call at 4pm and was told they had no powered sites left. I rang Amy back and she said no they had not sold the site and when I told her what my brother in law was just told, she very smartly told me they closed at 4 and they weren’t on site to sell anything.

She is a liar. They close at 5pm and I could also hear someone else in the back ground checking in people. Great service Amy. I hope you never have a medical mishap on your travels, and if you do you are treated the same as you treat your customers, with tone, attitude and you are a liar!
Great CP. Right on the beach. Great spot to explore from.

Stayed 2 nights in a 2 bedroom cabin.  Door on bunk room off track. couldn't move it.  Told it does that.  How about fix it?  Lounge settee absolutely disgusting.  It was filthy.  Curtains also very dirty. Needs a deep clean or thrown out. How about a shelf in bathroom?  Nowhere to put anything and so small constantly bumping the walls.  Frying pans were scratched to hell and one was still filthy. Linen and floors were clean.  Camp kitchen redeeming feature. Not worth the money.

Stayed here in our van and it was a great experience. Really clean facilities, beach is so close to sight
would recommend to anyone who wants to stay near Hobart
Close to beach and not far from hobart lots of little villages up and down the coast
Just spent 5 nights at Snug BeachCaravan Park. Lovely park right on beach front with dog beach close by. Thank you so much to Amy and Brad for a most enjoyable stay.

We’ve just finished a week long stay at Snug Beach Cabin & Caravan Park.

Every place we’ve stayed so far in Tassie has been nice, but this place was just lovely. Great site with plenty of room, amenities always clean & tidy, laundry room had a coin machine (big bonus!!). The owners were just lovely to deal with.

The beach is a short walk away & there’s a great playground for the kids too not far away too. IGA within walking distance.

If you are looking for a base to explore southern Tassie & the Hobart area, I 10/10 recommend them!

I found the cabins very clean and comfortably management are lovely friendly and helpful  I found the park  quiet and well set out and clean I recommend it to everyone the beach only a few steps from the park thank you for having me and my granddaughter she loved the fact that it was free wifi thank u again

Very friendly place. Generous hosts. My son’s favourite campsite in all of Tasmania!
Hosts were very forgiving when we hit our tennis ball into their yard, not just once but twice!
Thanks Brad and Amy for a lovely week near Hobart and for Xmas dinner
Beachfront caravan park in a quiet location. Some sites too small for large rigs. Amenities were very good. Great place to explore the Hobart region from.

This was the worst experience we have ever had in Tasmania. Not only was the owner’s wife rude, unpleasant and uncaring she was also not very bright. We had already stayed 6 nights when my husband became ill necessitating ongoing medical. This unexpected expense meant that I had to ask for a little more time to pay the last 2 days.This would not have resulted in any financial loss. I  was rudely refused and my explanatilon was cut off. She then harangued me for losing her money. I was supposed to ring her from the medical facility while my husband was undergoing treatment and I did not know the costs.so she could get other guests. At no time did she worry that there would be no other accommodation for us late on New Years Eve. We are elderly country pensioners not Bonnie and Clyde. Shame on you.

Great spot, very friendly staff, toilets/shower excellent. Let us extend our stay at short notice. Put on a free meal for everyone on Xmas eve & going to do the same on NYE, couldn’t ask for a better place to stay
Awesome caravan park. Free showers and lovely beach.
This is a great park to stay at . We stayed one night on the way through. Amenities super clean. Right by the water . Owners so friendly . Nice cabins .Recommend the park.  very happy .

Was an okay caravan park
Lots of permanent residents and the park has a bit of an identity crisis of being a caravan park or trailer park. Didn't enjoy camping next to a permanent caravan.
Amenities were plentiful, and everything worked fine. Short walk to the beach.

We really enjoyed our stay at Snug. It'a a great caravan park! The staff were polite and accommodating. The sites were generous in size. The ammentities were fantastic, and always clean.

The laundry was well equipped, including a notes to coin exchanger, along with a card to coin facility as well. I noted they even had a dog wash! A great park that we wouldn't hesitate to recommend.

In my travels in different countries I spent hundreds of days on campingrounds but I've never had such a good experience as in this one. First of all the location for our tent was amazing. We had a spot directly next to the access to the beach with a great view for every morning when we opened the tent. The kitchen was awesome, especially because of the fire that you could do every day that made the room warm but also comfortable and cosy. Everything is clean and tidy and the staff takes much care of the place with a good eye for the detail. But what impressed me most was how friendly, understanding, helpful and caring everyone was in this place. You can always talk with someone in the office and they always help you out in every situation. When I went into the office to ask for a favour about our stay, she did not only gave us this favour, she also saw that I had some problems with my hand. She asked what it was about and I answered that I cut myself with a knife, it was pretty deep. So she immediately looked for a first aid kit to disinfect the wound, put strips on it and cover it in a professional and good way.

I don't know what to say, I just never had such an experience. It was so kind, so caring, so helpful, so understanding. I can only recommend everyone to go this place if you are around and look for a nice campinground to stay. Thank you for everything!

Very friendly staff, top location right next to the beach and not far to Hobart central. The amenities and communal kitchen were perfect for camping and the hot showers were premium. Great stay, we will be back!

Had a powered site at snug with our family and camper trailer.
Really clean and well kept park
We especially appreciated the amazing bathrooms and high pressure showers with little heaters. It was a real luxury after camping in the bush with perfect green grass to camp on.
From here we explored Hobart Mt field national park  huon valley Mt wellington.
Snug is nice and quiet with everything you need really liked the local butcher also

I ended up in this accommodation last minute and I was pleasantly surprised by it. For a very reasonable price you get a very comfortable cabin with its own bathroom and kitchen. Apart from this they have a common kitchen with bbq, fireplace, sofas, etc. Overall great experience, the staff was very friendly and I had a very comfortable stay! Would recommend:)
We booked a powered site and this place had everything we needed! Laundry (need to pay), Showers, Toilets, Washing Line, and a recreation room with TV, Fridge, and DVD’s to borrow.

Stayed 2 nights. It was raining and very windy but I was snuggled in with my wine, book, bickies, cheese and Pate'! Great little cabin! Electric blankets, reverse cycle heating and smart tv with all the apps!!
Great location, service and value for money. The room was warm, clean and comfortable, we’ll definitely be back.
Lovely people great place great views
Très bon camping proche de Hobart, hôtes très sympathiques et très bonnes installations.

Great caravan park, with awesome facilities and an ideal location. The park was mostly empty, so we took the liberty to park nearby the facilities to make it easy to unload stuff for dinner, but due to the whereabouts of our campsite this was deemed against the rules. The manager or owner made us very uncomfortable and was extremely rude about having done what I would class as a harmless mistake, especially considering nobody came to that campsite we parked in.
After we agreed to move and apologised, she continued to interrogate us, including a further text message. It was quite over the top.
Upsetting experience, will not return.

Fantastic caravan park. Beach right behind us, excellent amenities lovely and clean, and staff were very helpful and were right about how good the Chinese down the road was. Very peaceful and quiet. Thank you for the wonderful stay.
One of the best caravan parks we have stayed in. Management very friendly, amenities spotless, camp kitchen well equiped and very clean
Very close to shops, beach and tourists attractions
A lovely little campground. The communal kitchen/lounge area is well equipped. The bathrooms are a little dated, but in good condition
Lovely caravan park. Clean, well maintained, great location
Toller Standort. Direkt am Strand. Super freundlich und hilfsbereit. Die sanitären Einrichtungen super sauber und gepflegt.
Bester Campingplatz den wir in Tasmanien besucht haben. Sauber und Tolle Campingküche und super Duschen und Toiletten! Die Lage am Strand war genial

One of the best parks we have stayed in.  Location to beach & shops.  The staff were very friendly & helpful.  Facilities excellent always clean & tidy & ensuites at top of park a great idea.  4.5 stars 🌟 Thank you
Wonderful staff, great facilities, laundry and bathrooms immaculate. Lovely gardens. Easy walk to IGA. One of the best parks we have stayed at
Great caravan park close to the way waters edge. Clean amenities and camp kitchen stocked with helpful utensils, and equipment. Extended our stay to continue exploring the peninsula.
Have not stayed there yet but I've heard good things about it
An absolutely beautiful place to stay on our van with a short walk to the beach ensuite was so clean and well appointed and the staff were very friendly

A beautiful place to stay by the water with clean amenities and friendly staff.
A breath of fresh air, thanks for having us!
Highly recommend!
Thank you once again great Aussie celebrations great food cheers again Guy & Judy

This Caravan Park is excellent for an overnight stay. Located near Margate and Kettering/Bruny Island, it is easy to access the beach, nearby park and tennis courts, and the river. The town centre is only a few minutes walk away, allowing for easy accessibility.
Love this place. 😀 Nice beach. Great camping space. Great facilities in the camp kitchen.

We just spent nine days staying at snug Caravan Park beautiful place. The owners were very helpful. Answer the question we asked everyone was very friendly. I would not hesitate to go back there again.
Beautiful and well maintained caravan park on the water.

When my partner and I arrived at Snug Beach Cabin & Caravan Park we had planned to stay only one night. That night turned into four nights, then two more nights after two nights in Hobart, then another night after a night in Cockle Creek. Like a magnet we are inherently drawn to the warm, calm and cozy atmosphere of the park. And after two months of having tried nearly every campground and caravan park in the Eastern half of Australia and all of Tasmania, the Snug Beach Cabin & Caravan Park is without a doubt our favorite.

The amenities are exceptional: clean bathrooms, wifi, a nice kitchen with a comfortable sitting area, a fire place, etc. The location is perfect: right on the beach, an IGA just up the road, strategically situated for easy day trips to Bruny Island, Cockle Creek, and Hobart. If you are looking for somewhere serene for an extended stay or a place with everything you need while just passing through, then look no further than Snug Beach Cabin & Caravan Park!

Make sure you leave ample flexibility when planning your trip, as it is very likely you'll be finding yourself in Snug for longer than you expected.
It is $40 just for 1 night 2 people in one tent with out electricity
Great and lovely place and views. it's just unbelievable if you visit Hubert. You need to spend some time here to get some energy 😉
Will not recommend anyone

We enjoyed a 3 night stay in one of the river view cabins and it was awesome.
Clean and spacious
Comfy bed
Well appointed
Friendly management
Give it a test drive yourself.🥰
Do not recommend, toilet blocks were not very clean, and all we could smell the whole time we were there was the dumping point.

Clean and tidy
Good weekend getaway

I was sleeping in the car park next door when unfortunately I locked myself out of the car (inexperienced young backpacker) so I found refuge in the campsite and spent a few hours in the lovely kitchen heated by the stove.  The next morning weeping, I asked the owners for help who cheered me up and, understanding the situation, managed to open the car.  They were truly helpful and kind, my saviors whom I thank with all my heart :)
WERNT allowed to camp there, The website says we have to be 16 to camp by ourselves but as soon as we called they denied it and said you have to 18

Caravan park in a great location. Level sites. Great camp kitchen. Downside is that I felt like I was being filmed by all the security cameras constantly. They are literally everywhere!!! Very angry caretaker lady!? Her husband on the other hand was lovely and friendly.
Best place to stay, awesome amenities and location is beautiful! Very helpful and friendly staff! Wish they had a place like this up in QLD where i’m from!

This place is amazing!  Awesome hosts, facilities, view, location.  We were travelling from QLD & stayed here on our first night in Tasmania, loved the place so much came back here to end our holiday.  Amy & Brad are awesome & very accommodating.  Highly recommend them to anyone wanting somewhere to stay.
Lovely little park right on the beach and close to the Bruny island ferry. Very clean facilities and spacious sites.

This is by far the nicest caravan park I have stayed in. The showers are lovely and the kitchen area is outstanding.

The little things like plants in the bathroom. A tv with DVDs and a fireplace.

The beach and the river are hidden treasures.

My car broke down the next town over and RACV booked a cabin here for me through Amy and boy am I stoked.
This park is so clean quiet and just overall a nice place to stay.
Nice people here and very relaxed attitude. Would come back

A 'snug' and quiet park, beautifully located with clean and comfortable facilities, including the most lush camp kitchen I've yet visited. Check in and out was flawless and the Owners were brilliant when I accidentally left something behind. Highly recommended.
Fantastic, clean, and well equipped cabins. A fantastic stop for the family or for that single or couple traveller.
Great campground n great management, wonderful camp kitchen n fire place.
Beautiful spot on the water. walked from our van to the sand in 10 steps & watched the sunrise together. Nice facilities & friendly staff.
